right to go ahead with the work.


The property holders whose inter-
ests are situated in the district af-
fected claim that that portion of the
city would be ruined and that they
will sue the company for damages If
the measure passes the council and
the work is carried out.


According to the views Of other
citizens of the city, while perhaps
10000 might be injured for a short
time by granting the railroad the
right to regrade the streets, yet there
are 10000 or more others who would,
in time, be benefited by it. and in this
number is all Union Park. They will
undoubtedly be heard from later.
w. B. Roberts, who was especially
bitter against the proposed improve
ment of the streets, stated to AS
sistant General Counsel Chamberlain
of the Northern Pacific that, in his
opinion, the railroad ought to move
its freight stations, which is one of
the factors the railroad officials have
in mind in the regrade scheme, to the
outer limits of the city.
